exemplar.title: Finding the volume of revolution of a spinning top

detailedExemplarData.title: Finding the volume of revolution of a spinning top

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear sequential development through data collection, modelling, integration, and cost analysis
Effective use of headings, sub-headings, numbered tables and captioned figures
Well-communicated description of image scaling and coordinate import
Table 2 succinctly organizes the 13 function equations and domains
weaknesses: Abrupt transitions between key sections (e.g., from intersection points to volume formula)
Lengthy personal narrative in the introduction distracts from mathematical focus
Block presentation of 13 integrals is unwieldy and reduces readability
Bibliography lacks consistent in-text citation and uniform styling
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and logically developed, with clear organization and mostly relevant content. Minor digressions in narrative, occasional structural interruptions, and redundancy keep the score at 3 rather than 4.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations: tables, overlaid images, plotted points, and algebraic expressions
Logical ordering of arguments from data through interpolation to integration and results
Generally consistent and accurate use of mathematical notation (subscripts, Î  for volume)
weaknesses: Key computational steps (e.g., polynomial expansions, numerical integrations) are omitted, limiting transparency
Axes and units are sometimes unlabeled; reported values occasionally lack units
Misaligned equality signs under chains of integrals and inconsistent coordinate notation
Some formula components (P_j(x)) are introduced without definition
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication is clear with a good logical structure and effective multiple representations, but skipped steps, unit omissions, and minor notation inconsistencies place it at a solid 3 rather than the top band.

strengths: Strong personal motivation: modelling a cherished childhood toy
Use of non-syllabus Lagrange interpolation demonstrates initiative beyond routine tasks
Clear framing of the investigation around personal context and real-world cost estimation
weaknesses: Methodology relies heavily on standard software functions and published formulas
No exploration of alternative modelling approaches or validation strategies
Creativity is good but stops short of highly innovative or unconventional methods
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach, employing creative tools and context, but the reliance on standard techniques and absence of novel strategies place Personal Engagement at 2.

strengths: Acknowledgement of plotting errors and one suggestion to improve segmentation
Insightful proposal to extend the investigation by computing surface area of revolution
weaknesses: Reflection is superficial and does not quantify the impact of identified errors
Lacks deep analysis of assumptions, no alternative strategies or error estimates
Evaluation of outcomes is limited to restatement of results without broader implications or future planning
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reflection shows only limited depth and critical analysis, with brief mentions of strengths and weaknesses but no substantial evaluation or quantified impact, warranting a score of 1.

strengths: Mathematics (interpolation, piecewise linear functions, definite integrals) is directly relevant and effectively applied
Techniques are fully commensurate with SL level and go beyond with Lagrange interpolation
Correct connection of geometric calculations to real-world mass and cost estimation
Clear step-by-step slope/intercept calculations for key segments
weaknesses: Internal inconsistency between reported volumes (48.6 vs 46.8 cmÂ³) undermines accuracy
Typographical errors in polynomial factors (e.g., misplacement of decimal) affect coefficient derivation
Intermediate algebraic steps omitted for most sections, preventing full verification
Rationale for choosing 13 segments is not justified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration uses relevant mathematics comprehensively and at an appropriate level, demonstrating strong understanding and correct application. Minor errors, inconsistencies, and omitted steps prevent the full 6 but justify a high score of 5.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are laid out in a logical order: data â interpolation â piecewise functions â domain restrictions â integral set-up â numerical results.  Explanations are usually clear enough for a reader to follow, although several computational steps (e.g. lengthy polynomial expansions, numerical integration outputs) are skipped, limiting full transparency.  The logical structure is good but not exemplary.

Derivations of sample polynomials, slope calculations and integrals indicate a sound understanding, yet several numerical values appear inconsistent (46.8 cmÂ³ vs 48.6 cmÂ³; rounded coefficients).  Intermediate algebraic steps for 11 of the 13 sections are omitted, preventing full verification.  Thus understanding is good with minor errors, falling into the mid-range band rather than higher.

The student employs tables of coordinates, several labelled graphs (original image, point cloud, piecewise curves), and algebraic expressions.  These representations complement each other and make the modelling understandable, though some graphs are rather small and the final 3-D concept is only implied.  The use is therefore effective but not outstanding.

The report moves from a personal narrative, through data collection, algebraic modelling, calculation of volumes and finally cost analysis in a clear sequential order.  Each section leads naturally to the next and there are no major logical gaps; however, transitions are sometimes abrupt (e.g. the sudden switch from obtaining intersection points to the volume formula).  Minor digressions in the introduction and conclusion slightly interrupt the flow, keeping the coherence at a solid rather than exemplary level.

Employing image scaling, point sampling, piecewise interpolation and cost analysis shows creativity and initiative above basic expectations.  However, most steps rely on readily available software functions and published formulas, and no alternative methods are explored, so the creativity is good but not exceptional.

Critical analysis is basic: the student lists one weakness (point placement) and one strength (sectioning) without deeper discussion of their impact, without alternative strategies, and without cross-checking the numerical result.  This corresponds to a rudimentary level of analysis.

The student simply restates the obtained volume and derived cost, asserting success.  There is no comparison to expected values, manufacturing tolerances, or follow-up plan.  Consequently the evaluation of outcomes is limited.

Reflection is limited to a short acknowledgement that mis-placed points could have introduced error and that splitting the shape into 13 parts âmight haveâ improved accuracy.  The comments remain superficial; they do not quantify error or examine assumptions.  Hence only limited reflection is evident.

Mathematical symbols, indices and functions are generally used correctly (e.g. Î  notation for volume, clear use of subscripts).  Minor lapses occur: units are sometimes omitted, the equality signs under the long chain of integrals are mis-aligned, and some coordinates are written inconsistently.  Overall, language and notation are mostly appropriate and consistent.

The investigation is clearly framed around the studentâs own experience and need (replicating the toy and estimating cost), demonstrating a personal approach.  Still, methodology is fairly conventional for IA modelling and there is limited evidence of novel strategy, placing it comfortably in the âclear evidenceâ but not âhighly innovativeâ range.

Most of the writing is relevant to modelling, yet lengthy storytelling in the introduction and repeated explanations of standard formulas add wordiness.  The exposition of every single integral limit could be condensed.  Consequently, the account is largely pertinent and reasonably concise, but there is still noticeable redundant detail.

All mathematics (interpolation, linear functions, definite integrals) directly supports modelling the top and estimating cost, showing effective relevance.  Each technique contributes meaningfully to the objective, and no substantial irrelevant mathematics is included.

Choosing to model a personal childhood toy and applying a non-syllabus Lagrange interpolation show meaningful initiative beyond classwork, indicating significant independent thinking.  Nonetheless, most calculations follow standard techniques from the course or GeoGebra outputs, so the level stops short of being outstanding.

Headings, sub-headings, numbered tables/figures and a short conclusion give the exploration a recognisable structure that is easy to follow.  Tables precede the discussion they support and figures are captioned.  A few items (e.g. the two integration result lines) appear before an explanation and the bibliography is not consistently referenced in-text, so the overall organisation is good but not exceptional.

The work involves calculus (volume by disks) and polynomial interpolation, both well within or slightly beyond SL level.  All mathematics is appropriate and fully commensurate with the course, though not deployed with the sophistication expected for higher marks (e.g. numerical integration handled by technology without justification of method).

annotations.comment: The block of 13 integrals is presented as a continuous list, making it hard to follow individual contributions; consider a more concise tabular or numbered format.
Label each integral with its corresponding function name (e.g., Ïâ«[f(x)]Â² dx for h(x)) to improve traceability and clarity.
The volume text reports 48.6 cmÂ³ but then uses 46.8 in mass computation, creating an internal inconsistency that must be resolved.
Volume values should consistently include units (cmÂ³) when reported in text to maintain mathematical communication standards.
Introduction contains extensive personal narrative that, while engaging, distracts from the mathematical focus. Consider condensing background details to maintain conciseness and relevance.
Strong personal context framing the aim of replicating the childhood toy demonstrates clear personal engagement and motivation for this investigation.
Axes and units are not labeled when scaling the image in GeoGebra, reducing mathematical clarity and precision in the model setup.
The description of importing and resizing the image to a 1:1 scale is clearly communicated and well organized.
Notation for point B reads â(0.22),(0.18)â instead of a single ordered pair, which may cause confusion when interpolating.
The large coordinate table spans pages and interrupts narrative flow; consider condensing or relocating extensive data to an appendix for coherence.
Combining plotted points, image overlays, and a coordinate table provides an effective multiple-representation approach to outline the spinning top.
Lagrange formula is introduced without defining the component terms P_j(x), which hinders full comprehension of the interpolation process.
Implementing Lagrange interpolation goes beyond the standard SL syllabus and demonstrates creative initiative in modeling the curve segments.
In the h(x) calculation, factors appear as (xâ0.59) instead of (xâ0.059), indicating a typographical error that affects coefficient derivation.
The rationale for dividing the shape into exactly 13 sections is not discussed; consider explaining how segmentation balances accuracy and complexity.
The step-by-step calculation of slope and intercept for s2(x) is well structured and shows good mathematical communication.
Narrow domains on near-vertical linear segments result in graph clutter; consider merging or annotating these segments differently for clarity.
Domain endpoints in Table 2 are truncated to different decimal places (e.g., 0.914 vs 0.9143), which undermines precision in mathematical communication.
Table 2 succinctly consolidates 13 function equations with domains, effectively organizing complex information for the reader.
Density is stated as âcmÂ³/gâ but should read âg/cmÂ³â for correct unit representation of mass per volume.
Applying mass = volume Ã density correctly connects geometric calculation to real-world cost estimation.
Evaluation notes plotting errors but does not quantify their impact on volume accuracy; deeper critical reflection is needed.
Proposing the calculation of surface area via the formula for revolution shows insightful extension and reflection on further modeling.
Bibliography entries lack in-text citations, making it difficult to trace sources back to specific content in the report.
The bibliographyâs range of sourcesâfrom software documentation to calculus referencesâreflects thorough research and presentation.
Final bibliography entry format is inconsistent; apply uniform styling (e.g., italics for website titles) to improve presentation.
